theme: Employee 100% demo coverage — a hero of the EMPLOYEE/member vantage (Maya Chen, DevOps Engineer @ Cervato Systems), logged into a stack from a FRESH demo-up, sees a believable fully-populated person + catalog on EVERY reachable page: real seeded content (not placeholder/empty-state), substantial per-section cardinality, a self-consistent persona (role <-> curated skills <-> bio <-> a real-photo avatar consistent across menu + profile <-> work history <-> recent activity), an org with a name + logo, and ZERO prod-eject escapes. The gate was RE-SCOPED 2026-06-25 (user live-review) from a weak DOM-text-density check (textLen>40, which green-lit placeholder cards + nav chrome) to this semantic believability bar. The milestone delivers BOTH the iteration protocol (corpus/ops/demo/coverage-protocol.md) AND the Playwright semantic-coverage harness (rext stack-verify/e2e/ — the first non-Go rext dev/test dependency), then iterates root-cause fixes into rext (stack-seeding seeders / stack-snapshot serve-grants + sim-embeddings replay / the demo injection + Sentinel-reload + stale-CLI reproducibility fixes / crawl-scope + allow-rules) until the employee gate is GREEN and REPRODUCIBLE on a fresh zero-manual demo-up. Tooling + docs only — ZERO platform-repo edits.

benchmarks: n/a — no performance-sensitive hot path. The seeders are DB-IO-bound bulk COPY + deterministic string-builders over bounded palettes; the sim-embeddings replay is a bounded COPY + a single pgvector reindex; the Playwright harness is wall-clock-bound by the live stack, gated by a count (gateMet), not a snapshot-diff. No benchmark target.
